title: Decadent Yeasty Mexican Pozole Bisque
description: A rich, creamy Mexican comfort dish featuring a yeasty flavor profile, made with speculaas spice mix, and tailored to be diabetic-friendly.
instructions: Sauté the chopped onion in a large Dutch oven over medium heat until translucent, about 5 minutes.
Add the minced garlic and cook for an additional 1-2 minutes, until fragrant.
Add the chicken breast pieces and cook until browned on all sides and cooked through, about 5-7 minutes.
Add the chipotle peppers in adobo sauce, Mexican oregano, yeast, cumin, and cayenne pepper, and stir to combine.
Add the canned hominy, chicken broth, and browned chicken to the pot, and bring to a boil.
Reduce the heat to low and simmer, covered, for 20-25 minutes, or until the hominy is tender.
Use an immersion blender or a regular blender to puree the soup until smooth.
Return the pot to the stovetop and stir in the heavy cream over low heat until warmed through.
Season the soup with salt and pepper to taste, and serve hot.
Garnish with chopped fresh cilantro, scallions, or a dollop of sour cream, if desired.
